Chapter 7: Season 1 Chapter 7: Season 3

Overview

This article is about the second season of Chapter 7. For other uses of Season 2, see Season 2 (disambiguation).

Chronological Order
← Previous Next →
Final Wave April Fools
Chapter 7: Season 2, also known as Chapter Seven: Season 2 and Season 40, was the fortieth season of Fortnite: Battle Royale and the second season of Chapter 7. The season began on March 19th 2026 and ended on June 5th 2026 immediately following the Shattered Live Event.

The season's main slogan is SHOWDOWN, with the theme of one-on-one Rivalries. The storyline features a clash between The Foundation and The Ice King.

Previously, in Fortnite...

Previously, in Fortnite...[]

Since the Collider's destruction, The Foundation mysteriously went missing. At some point, he was captured by the Ice King and frozen in his private collection.

After receiving a distress signal from Jones while analyzing the aftermath of the Battle of Oninoshima, The Order traveled to the Golden Coast but was intercepted by the Dark Voyager, who seized the Zero Point Shard as part of a mission to recover other Shards and rebuild the original Zero Point. With the Looper's help, the Order escaped the Dark Voyager's prison and contacted the remaining members of the Seven, leading them to the Golden Coast using schematics left behind by the Scientist. The first member to arrive, however, is a version of the Visitor from an Reality: Unknown onboard a rocket. Later on in that reality, the Ice King ushered in a massive snowstorm that blanketed the Athena island in snow.

Leadup to Season 2

Leadup to Season 2[]

Main Article: The Reassembly Events and Final Wave

The Order and the Looper infiltrate the Dark Voyager's Labs and find a single encrypted message, which AMIE deciphers as one to a master the Dark Voyager is working for. Inside his Dark Harvester spaceship, the Dark Voyager continuously traveled to other realities in search of other Zero Point Shards, collecting two in the process. In order to find more, the Dark Harvester fired a laser blast at the Shards, which led what currently constituted the Zero Point to create three Pure Portals that were linked to the realities that housed the other Shards. One of these Portals opened in an icy tundra overlooking an ominous castle.

As the shard distortions appear across the Golden Coast, The Foundation arrives in Reality: 266 joining the New Seven and, after getting rid of his old one, reforged tech suits are built for him and The Order. But just as the team of heroes is mostly reunited, three Zero Point Shards are brought on the Island, bringing different realities and merging them with the Island. The Ice King and his Frigid Fortress cover the northern part of the Island, Reality: 7021's Squibbly Shores takes the place of Bumpy Bay while, near the New Sanctuary, a shard contaminates the waters near Looper's Leap.

The Ice King poses himself as a new threat for the Golden Coast, looking to freeze whoever holds enough power, deeming them unworthy. Among his collection there are the restless spirit Orelia, the warrior Guan Yu, the first Avenger Captain America and The Prisoner, his former knight. While on the Island, the Ice King adds Human Bill and Peely to his throne room prisoners.

In the meantime, after flying multiple times above the skies of The Elites' headquarters within Reality: 337, the Dark Voyager lands his Dark Harvester on the ground to analyze his surroundings, but moments before the ship could take off to return to Reality: 266, Jules secretly boards it. Once on the Golden Coast, Jules joins the New Seven, considering the Dark Voyager a threat to all life and The Elites' very values.

Story

Story[]

The New Seven plan a first strike against the Ice King, but The Foundation is frozen in the process and locked inside the Ice King Vault. Meanwhile, the Dark Voyager decided to forge an alliance with the Ice Kingdom's ruler. Arriving at Frigid Fortress, he is allowed to walk across the room by the Frozen Knights and presents his offer to the Ice King in return for his help, which involved using the Ice Kingdom's power to keep the elements in sync[1]. The Ice King accepted the Voyager's offer and began to help the him in his quest to rebuild The Zero Point.

The New Seven and their allies launched a mission to locate the Foundation with a sensor device and activate his recovered suit's device to melt the ice and set him free. As The Order, The Visitor, Jones and Hope find their way through the icy winds, The Foundation is freed from ice and, after taking down one of the Ice King's Frozen Knights, he makes his way through the Fortress' throne room. Once there, a confrontation began and the leader of the Seven dodges ice beams shot by the Ice King, destroying Peely's frozen body in the process and freeing Human Bill from his ice pillar, but the confrontation ends in a tie.

Upon the liberation of The Foundation, the Dark Voyager and the Ice King devised a plan to distract him and The Seven. While the Ice King would begin a "personal" war with The Foundation, forcing the Islanders to choose sides between the Ice Kingdom and The New Seven, The Dark Voyager would be free to move around and collect and fuse the Shards. In the meantime, The New Seven want on their own mission, Hope and The Order would stay behind at New Sanctuary to get things into place, Jones, The Looper and The Foundation would stay in the terrain and do the fight, while The Visitor patrols and studies the shards.

Jones and the Foundation[]

In the first week of the showdown between the New Seven and the Ice Kingdom, The Looper is called to assist Jones and his "best friend", The Foundation. After personally vouching for The Looper, Jones tasks them with guiding Foundation and helping him understand the severity of the Zero Point Shards situation. Once Foundation realizes how critical it is, he insists on assessing their weaknesses and leverage points alongside Jones and The Looper.

While studying Frigid Fortress, Jones questions how Foundation could possibly escape such a place. Shortly after, the three engage in target practice to test the new Vector 7 DMR, with Foundation actively participating. Jones also takes the opportunity to showcase a new trick using the Skyline Deployer.

Later, Jones and The Looper team up again, and Jones casually asks whether Foundation is available. Foundation agrees to join them, though only in exchange for bars. Jones uses the moment to offer him a few "gifts", including a weapon, a music playlist, and what he calls an "early advantage". At the end of their mission, Jones and The Looper regroup with Order to power up the Holotable. Using the voice password "Sanctuary", Order accesses the status of all members of The Seven and connects Foundation to their secure server.

The Foundation vs. The Ice King[]

During the following week, The Looper is tasked by The Foundation with testing mythic and exotic weapons for armory evaluation. The latter concluding they are still fun to use despite not being toys. The Foundation then plans an attack on the Ice King's collection. By destroying parts of it alongside The Looper, they hope to pressure the Ice King into leaving the fight, but instead only succeed in making him angrier.

Later, the duo begin studying the effects of the Zero Point Shards on the surrounding terrain. These anomalies create hazards such as icy footing and zones of low gravity, allowing them to gain new advantages in battle. Later, the both of them confront the Ice King once again, defeating him long enough to grant the Foundation a brief moment of peace. The mission ultimately concludes with The Looper searching the Island for notable individuals who might assist the New Seven.

Rebuilding with Hope and The Order[]

As enhancements to the New Sanctuary continue, Hope asks The Looper to help her and The Order with ongoing work. After The Looper gathers supplies and ammunition, Hope admits she wishes she could see what lies ahead so she could contribute more to the fight. Nonetheless, the work continues. After practicing shoulder riding to map common traversal routes and battle locations across the Island, The Looper hones their sniping skills. Hope notes that she had heard they were a contender with the Order, unlike her, and upon seeing their skills she asks them for advice.

Finally, alongside Hope, The Looper decrypts a recorded message from The Imagined taken from one of their old conversations during Chapter 3: Season 2, in the middle of the war against the Imagined Order, where she ultimately died. In this message, the Imagined speaks about remembering why they fought. The Order notes that she will remember it for both of them, while Hope remarks that she wishes she had gotten the chance to know her, adding that she feels lucky to have met her sister, The Order.

Foundation vs Ice King: The Showdown Intensifies[]

Later, The Looper is then send by The Foundation to eliminate the Ice King's knights. Shortly afterward, while the Foundation mentions the need for his Field General, The Looper goes to the Holotable to decrypt one of the recorded conversations they once had with The Origin, also during Chapter 3: Season 2, during the war against the Imagined Order. In doing so, they reveal the Origin's current status to the members of the Seven, who learn that their Field General was lost to the Chrome. The news saddens both the Visitor and the Order, but also the Foundation, who turns off his communications for a while, far more affected by the revelation than he would like to admit.

A Voyager's Arrival[]

While the Dark Voyager continued searching for the final Zero Point Shard across other realities. One of these journeys led him to the Storm-ravaged world inhabited by the Homebase heroes, where his investigations were hindered by the world's defenders. At the same time, several Homebase survivors, including Penny, Jess, Ray, and Lars, arrived in Reality: 266, where they decided to assist the New Seven as long as they're here, establishing additional fortifications near Looper's Leap before working to return to their own reality with the help of The Looper.

Squibbly vs. Fishstick[]

As the Foundation keeps fighting the Ice King, The Looper gets involved in Squibbly joined the ranks of the Ice King alongside his people while Fishstick joined The Foundation. Squibbly worked to reduce Fishtick's supply and scare away customers from his restaurant, Sticks, greatly impacting it.

In the following week, facing a dangerous situation, Fishstick nevertheless found an ally. One of the Chef Squibblies decided to investigate on Fishstick following the common thought of his people considering him evil. However, he found out everything was a misunderstanding. Considering the Sticks restaurant profitable and needing a chef, he joined Fishstick, getting hired by Sticks and managed to turn things around by offering new dishes made with "synthetic squids," allowing the restaurant to recover and experience a surge in customers.

At the same time, however, the disappearance of the Squibbly Chef who helped Fishstick, now dubbed the Missing Ink, along with Sticks' rising sales, worried his fellow Squibblies. They investigated Sticks' calamari menu by looking for the Dine and Splash bags before searching any clue for the Missing Ink. A confrontation happened with the Dread Punisher Squibbly but he knew nothing at all, leaving the Squibbly Chef out of lead.

Shattered[]

The Foundation and the Ice King prepare for their final showdown. Both are awaiting the Dark Voyager's return to Reality: 266 with the final Zero Point Shard, with the Foundation seeking not only to prevent the Zero Point's reconstruction, but also to enact personal revenge on the Ice King.

Team Foundation and Team Ice King meet at the Zero Point in Dark Dominion. The Foundation aims his rifle at the Dark Harvester only for it to malfunction, and Jones realizes they had been lured into a trap. Both teams erupt fire on each other, with Team Foundation succeeding in disabling the Harvester. The Foundation and the Ice King then engage in a head-on fight, and when Jones reminds the Foundation of his mission to escort the Shard away, he instead uses it as a weapon to deflect attacks from the Ice King's Infinity Blade, which begins to tear the Island apart. The Infinity Blade becomes lodged in the Shard and turns into a flock of Rift Butterflies.

The Looper's soul is projected into either the Foundation or the Ice King (depending on their allegiance) as their brawl continues, damaging parts of Dark Dominion. When the Foundation attempts to finish the Ice King off, the Ice King freezes the Foundation into ice, having won the Showdown. The Dark Voyager meets with the Ice King and, declaring his duties fulfilled, obliterates him.

The final Shard is reunited with the Zero Point, which is then blasted with an intense beam of energy by the Harvester. Jones signals his team to retreat as the Zero Point begins to go haywire, shattering the Island as reality is warped. A Rift Butterfly delivers the Infinity Blade to the Looper, which they then use to slice the Harvester in two.

Afterwards, the Dark Voyager visits Jones and the Visitor, who reveals that this was his plan all along. He betrays Jones and reveals himself as Geno, the founder of the Imagined Order and the Dark Voyager's master.

Battle Pass

Battle Pass[]

Due to the price increases on V-Bucks that went into effect at the start of the season, the Showdown Battle Pass costs 800 V-Bucks for the first time, with an additional 25-level boost being offered for 1,800 V-Bucks. Fortnite Crew subscribers immediately unlock the Battle Pass alongside the OG Pass, Music Pass, and LEGO® Pass. Battle Pass levels require 80,000 , or up to 99 rewards can be claimed individually for 150 V-Bucks each.

The total amount of V-Bucks obtainable from the Battle Pass were reduced as well, from 1,500 V-Bucks to 800 V-Bucks, and V-Bucks rewards are no longer offered in the Bonus Rewards track of the Battle Pass.

From the season's launch on March 19th to March 26th, obtaining the Battle Pass will also unlock the Stealth Tech Foundation style for The Foundation (Reforged) Outfit. At the beginning of Chapter 7: Season 3, an additional style for Exalted Ice King was awarded to all Showdown Battle Pass owners after Team Ice King won the Showdown over Team Foundation.

  • Squibbly
  • Lady Windfrost
  • Dasha
  • The Foundation (Reforged)
  • The Order (Reforged)
  • Elite Jules
  • Bugs Bunny

Map Updates

Map Updates[]

Update v40.00

March 19th 2026

  • The Dark Voyager and the Ice King have joined forces to oppose the newly-reformed Seven.
    • The locales that were tethered to the Zero Point were brought over to the Golden Coast, bringing new Named Locations:
      • The Ice King's Frigid Fortress replaces Clawsy Lodge.
      • Squibbly Shores replaces Bumpy Bay.
    • In addition, existing locations on the Golden Coast were given makeovers:
      • The Seven establish their base, New Sanctuary, at what once was Classified Canyon.
      • The Dark Voyager's Dark Dominion is a renovated version of Lethal Labs.
  • Latte Landing has been covered in snow and ice.
  • The Dark Harvester has disappeared.
  • Cave in the redwood biome is now empty.
  • A drag strip was added on the streets of Sandy Strip.
  • Sets at Sus Studios and Peelian Swamp were changed.
  • Big Shots at Battlewood Boulevard is now fully constructed.
  • Chappell Roan apartment at Battlewood Boulevard was changed.
  • The Shape is now blue.
  • All Hot Air Balloon stations were removed.
    • 3 of them were replaced by new Seven outposts.

Update v40.10

April 1st 2026

  • Captain America broke free from one of the pillars in Frigid Fortress.
  • Ski Lodge huts on the mountain near Frigid Fortress were removed.

Update v40.20

April 16th 2026

  • Builder's Barracks has been added near Swift Shard.
  • Tiptop Terrace has been chopped down, being replaced by Treechopped Timbermill.
  • Sticks has a new sign displaying 99 sold meals.
  • Few Castle buildings around the Ice Biome were removed.
  • Gas Station in the Ice Biome was changed.
  • A plushie of Jemma was added on the stage in Wonkeeland.

April 25th 2026

3:54 PM ET

  • Builder's Barracks has been expanded due to Team Foundation reaching Milestone #2 in Act II of the Showdown Event.

Update v40.30

April 30th 2026

  • Elites Outpost has appeared southwest of Wonkeeland.
  • Sticks sign now displays 999,999,999 sold meals.

9:00am ET

  • A new golden rift has begun to form above Dark Dominion.

2:00pm ET

  • The golden rift has opened.

May 1st 2026

  • Frozen Reforged Infinity Blade can now be found in the room behind the throne.

3:00pm ET

  • The Dark Harvester has returned through the rift, and is descending the fourth Zero Point Shard into place.

May 2nd 2026

3:30pm ET

  • The fourth Zero Point Shard is now in place.

May 3rd 2026

3:30pm ET

  • A new golden rift has opened, and the Dark Harvester has left.

May 4th 2026

2:00pm ET

  • The golden rift has closed.

3:30pm ET

  • The golden rift has disappeared.

May 9th 2026

12:35 AM ET

  • Slipstream has been added in the Snow Biome around Frigid Fortress due to Team Ice King reaching Milestone #4 in Act II of the Showdown Event.

5:23 PM ET

  • The Vortex Beacon at the main base of New Sanctuary has been permanently open due to Team Foundation reaching Milestone #4 in Act II of the Showdown Event.

Update v40.40

May 14th 2026

  • New landmarks based on Overwatch were added, including:
    • Busan Sanctuary
    • Hanamura Courtyard
    • King's Row Tower
    • Watchpoint bases inspired by Watchpoint: Gibraltar map were added, including:
      • Watchpoint: Battlewood
      • Watchpoint: Frigid
    • Along with Pump and Run Gas Stations were replaced by watchpoint bases, including:
      • Watchpoint: Sandy
      • Watchpoint: Squibbly
    • Overwatch Launch Pads were added, including:
      • Overwatch Landing Pad Alpha
      • Overwatch Landing Pad Epsilon
      • Overwatch Landing Pad Omega
      • Overwatch Landing Pad Zeta
  • Behind the Battlewood Sign, The Seven are starting to dig a hole for a bunker.
  • Lexa's Twisted Fate Blade has appeared in a container in Elites Outpost.
  • The golden rift above Elites Outpost has closed.

May 21st 2026

5:30 AM ET

  • A new golden rift has begun to form above Swift Shard.

4:30 PM ET

  • The golden rift has opened.

May 22nd 2026

9:00 AM ET

  • The Dark Harvester has appeared through the rift, and is attracting Swift Shard with its tractor beam.
    • The rift has closed.

May 23rd 2026

9:00 AM ET

  • The Dark Harvester has successfully extracted Swift Shard and is lowering it into place in Dark Dominion alongside with The Dark Voyager.
    • Swift Shard has been replaced by an echo of itself.

3:30 PM ET

  • The Dark Voyager has further descended down the tractor beam, rotating and lowering the fifth Zero Point Shard with him.
  • A golden rift has begun to form above the Dark Harvester.

May 24th 2026

9:00 AM ET

  • The fifth Zero Point Shard is in place, and The Dark Voyager has retreated back into his ship and closed the hatch.
  • The golden rift has opened.

May 25th 2026

9:00 AM ET

  • The Dark Harvester has went through the rift.
    • The rift then disappeared.

Update v40.41

May 28th 2026

4:30 PM ET

  • A new golden rift has begun to form above Anti-Grav Shard.

May 29th 2026

4:00 AM ET

  • The golden rift has opened.

9:00 AM ET

  • The Dark Harvester has appeared through the rift, and is attracting Anti-Grav Shard with its tractor beam.

3:30 PM ET

  • The Dark Harvester has successfully extracted Anti-Grav Shard.
    • Anti-Grav Shard has been replaced by an echo of itself.

9:00 PM ET

  • The Dark Harvester is lowering the sixth Zero Point Shard into place in Dark Dominion alongside with The Dark Voyager.
  • A golden rift has begun to form above the Dark Harvester.

May 30th 2026

3:30 PM ET

  • The sixth Zero Point Shard is in place, and The Dark Voyager has retreated back into his ship and closed the hatch.
  • The golden rift has opened.

May 31st 2026

9:00 AM ET

  • The Dark Harvester has went through the rift.
    • The rift then disappeared.
  • The Seven's bunker has been dug out more, and excavators can be found inside.
    • A timer for Shattered has appeared directly above.
  • Another timer for Shattered has appeared above Frozen Shard.

The Showdown begins March 19! https://youtu.be/e4sCLYMSokg

March 18th 2026
The Showdown has begun!


Here’s how you choose a side and lead your team to victory, unlocking new weapons, items and more this Season.

March 19th 2026
This time... your choices matter. March 19th 2026

Ice Prince Sidekick
Ice King's Frostwalker Kicks

that fresh map feeling 💕 March 19th 2026

Media Description Date
The Ice King Returns
Frozen, but far from finished. 		March 9th 2026
New Fortnite Season Launches March 19
Settle the score starting Thursday, March 19. 		March 17th 2026
Fortnite: Showdown | March 19
The Ice King returns in Chapter 7 Season 2: Showdown March 19. 		March 17th 2026
Fortnite: Showdown | March 19
Take the Plunge in Chapter 7 Season 2: Showdown March 19. 		March 17th 2026
A Dark Plot Takes Shape | March 19
Chapter 7 Season 2: Showdown is coming March 19. 		March 17th 2026
New Season Coming March 19
Pick a side. Hold the line. Fortnite: Showdown launches March 19. 		March 18th 2026
Battle Pass - Bonus Outfit Style & Instant Unlock
Instantly unlock Exalted Ice King and get a bonus Foundation Style and accessories when you buy the Battle Pass or have an active Crew subscription. Available now through March 26 at 11:59 PM ET. 		March 19th 2026
See How You Stack Up!
Log in to the Fortnite Showdown Global Leaderboards via QR Code and get the “Original Rivalry” Spray. Climb the ranks for a chance at IRL rewards like GPUs, consoles, and more! 		March 19th 2026
The Showdown Begins Now!
Make your choice and settle the score… do you side with The Foundation or The Ice King? Choose your Team, win Rivalries, and climb the Leaderboard to unlock new loot!. 		March 19th 2026

Trivia

Trivia[]

  • Two teaser words were revealed for Chapter 7: Season 2:
    • Ted Timmins, Fortnite's design director, described the season with the word "Bearer".[2]
    • Mark Rein's word was "Skyzips".[3]
  • The trailers for Chapter 7: Season 2 references the trailers released to promote Avengers: Doomsday (2026).
    • Both Avengers: Doomsday and Chapter 7: Season 2 feature Steve Rogers.
    • Before the countdown at the end of each trailer, there is always a group of text in the format of [CHARACTER] will return to Fortnite: Showdown, similar to the Doomsday trailer but instead the character will return in Avengers: Doomsday.
    • A countdown appears at the end of some trailers for Chapter 7: Season 2, similar to the Doomsday countdown.
  • Chapter 7: Season 2...
    • ...was the first season to be officially split into multiple acts.
    • ...was the first season since Chapter 2: Season 7 to introduce an item that is directly from Fortnite: Save the World, that being the Super Shredder.
    • ...was the first season since Chapter 4: Season 3 to be delayed; it was originally going to start on March 4th 2026.
    • ...was the first season to feature a Community Day.
    • ...was the first season to include an April Fools gameplay update since 2018.
    • ...was the first season, that took place during May, to not unvault Lightsabers.
    • ...had the first Battle Pass that only included 800 V-Bucks.
    • ...had the first Battle Pass that granted a bonus reward for purchasing it during the first week of the season.
    • ...had the first Battle Pass, since Chapter 2: Season 2, where it was not possible to unlock everything in the pass. This is because the community had to choose between the white style for The Foundation (Reforged) or the Exalted Ice King, with the other becoming unobtainable.
  • To promote the Team Foundation vs. Team Ice King showdown, Epic Games collaborated with quarterback Fernando Mendoza, Olympic figure skater and gold medalist Alysa Liu, and HUNTR/X rapper-singer Rei Ami for a series of promotional posts in which they would pick a side. Mendoza chose Team Foundation, while Liu and Ami sided with Team Ice King.
  • Despite the Assault Rifle being vaulted in almost every rarity, it is still possible to obtain in Legendary due to the Midas Flopper.
References
